title: bermudiana apiculata
intro: Bermudiana apiculata, also known as Bermudian tea tree or Bermuda buttonwood, is a deciduous shrub native to the Caribbean and parts of the Americas, characterized by its small, greenish-yellow flowers and red berries. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include antibacterial and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help soothe skin irritations and wounds. The health benefits of Bermudiana apiculata include reducing inflammation, relieving digestive issues, and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and berries are often made into teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
